text.content:
Original PDF page 82

II

heroic
PATHS
Original PDF page 83

Original PDF page 84

Your Legendary Path

A

Barbarian who has spent their life on the
front lines of a celestial war develops much
differently from one who has hunted titans
across the elemental planes. A Wizard who
has perfected their casting to turn enemies
to cinders with fiery blasts is very unlike
another who has devoted their studies to
dark celestial bodies and is now able to conjure
miniature black holes. We created Heroic Paths to give
these distinctions a mechanical identity, representing
the specific history and interests of a character rather
than the general arc of their class archetype.

Heroic Paths are a series of features that bring varied
benefits, build upon each other in steps (one per level,
starting at level 21), and sometimes connect, allowing
you to jump between paths. Each has a specific flavor
and mechanical identity–it’s a progression system like
regular class levels, except that you get to choose them
now, twenty-one levels into the fray. Deepen the flavor
of your class, your chosen mythical techniques or spells,
or go a new direction! As long as you meet the
prerequisites, your Heroic Path is yours to define.

Your Legendary Subclass
Subclasses are one of the most varied and “custom” parts
of any D&D game–between obscure sourcebooks, thirdparty material, and your own homebrew content, no two
tables look the same, even when the base classes are
indeed the same. Writing a book that accounts for all of that
is unrealistic, especially considering the licensing
restrictions around third-party subclass content. Heroic
Paths are our answer to that problem: a flexible layer of
progression that works alongside whatever subclass your
character already has. Think about your Heroic Path as your
Legendary Subclass! We recommend choosing a Heroic Path
that complements your subclass’s flavor.

The Anatomy of Heroic Paths

Each Heroic Path is composed of two types of benefits:
Heroic Feats, the more powerful of the two, and
Heroic Perks, which connect them. You advance a
step in a Heroic Path each time you gain a level, starting
at the 21st. You can also start a new Heroic Path you
meet the prerequisites for, or branch out into another
Heroic Path when the step you’re on allows it. By the
end of the Legendary Levels, you will have made 5
steps.

Heroic Feat. Each Heroic Path is made up of 5 steps.
Steps 1, 3, and 5 are Heroic Feats–meaningful features
that will typically affect how your character plays in
combat. Heroic Feats are divided into three Tiers: each
Tier is considerably stronger than the last. A Tier I
Heroic Feat is unlocked at step 1, a Tier II at step 3, and
a Tier III at step 5. Put simply, the further up a Heroic
Path a Feat is, the stronger it will be.

Heroic Perk. Steps 2 and 4 of every Heroic Path are
Heroic Perks–smaller benefits which often allow your
character to jump between Heroic Paths.

When planning your progression, consider that
getting up to the highest steps will reward you with
mightier choices down the line. It might be tempting to
just start a new Heroic Path each time you level up, but
that might end up hindering your plans in the long run.

Heroic Path Progression

Level
Steps Taken

21st
1

22nd
2

23rd
3

24th
4

25th
5

Original PDF page 85

The Full Picture

When choosing a Heroic Path, consider the Heroic
Feats you hope to reach by the end of your progression.
Not all Legendary Campaigns will run to 25th level, so a
benefit you are building toward may never arrive. Some
combinations will also prove stronger in certain
campaigns, depending on what your DM puts in front of
you. That said, do not stress over making the most
optimized choice at every level. Whenever you level up,
think about how your character is growing within the
story you are telling. A flavorful choice will almost
always serve you better than a “perfect” one.

Heroic Paths are highly modular. With each new step,
you can advance your current path or start a fresh one.
By the end of a standard 23rd-level campaign, for
example, you will have advanced 3 steps–enough to
reach a Tier 2 Heroic Feat in a single path, or spread
across three different paths for three Tier 1 Heroic
Feats. A full 25th-level progression unlocks 5 steps,
enough to reach Tier 3 in a single path or build a wide
network of smaller benefits that synergize with one
another.

Remember that even building upon a single path
allows for choice. Just because you started the
Sovereign Heroic Path doesn’t mean you have to finish
it–you can always pivot into the Overlord Heroic Path at
the intersections offered by certain Heroic Perks.

Prerequisites & Branching Out

Each Heroic Path has certain prerequisites. Whatever
prerequisites are noted at the beginning of a Heroic
Path must be met to choose any step of said Heroic
Path. This means that, if you are accessing a Heroic
Path from a connected Heroic Perk, you must meet the
requirements of the new Heroic Path to do so.

Some Heroic Paths Prerequisites are class-specific–
only certain Legendary Classes may access those paths.
This means your character requires at least 20 levels of
that class to access it. Some are stat-based, meaning a
certain score is required for you to step into the Heroic
Path. And some have no prerequisites at all, meaning
any class can take them whenever they choose.

Each step of a Heroic Path, be it Perk or Feat,
requires the previous step of that Heroic Path, unless
you are accessing the Heroic Path through a connected
Heroic Perk from a different Heroic Path.

Custom Heroic Paths

Heroic Paths are designed to be modular and settingagnostic. If you want to create a path that reflects a
particular legend, culture, or character archetype in
your world, the following principles will help you keep it
balanced with the existing roster.

Identity. What fantasy does this path serve? Every path
in this chapter has a clear identity that does not overlap
with another. Before building, check the existing paths
and confirm your concept occupies its own mechanical

and narrative space. Sometimes, minor tweaks to
existing paths will serve you better than building from
scratch.

Tier Scaling. Tier I features should be meaningful but
relatively modest–lesser improvements to abilities,
situational advantages, or a single strong passive. Tier II
should introduce a meaningful shift in how the
character plays, comparable to a new level of the fourth
tier in non-Legendary D&D. Tier III should feel like the
culmination of the path’s identity, powerful enough to
justify the full step-by-step investment–it should carry
about the same impact as the strongest mythical
techniques, only with lesser costs to take advantage of.

Heroic Perks. Perks sit between Tiers and should do
three things: add a smaller benefit that reinforces the
path’s identity, create flavor, and optionally serve as a
bridge into a connected path. A Perk that is too
powerful overshadows the Heroic Feats it surrounds.

Prerequisites. Be deliberate about who can access
your path. Class-based prerequisites create focused,
flavorful paths (more like subclasses), while stat-based
prerequisites are more flexible. No-prerequisite paths
should be constructed with a slightly lower power
ceiling, since they are available to every character
regardless of class or build.

Benchmarking. Compare your Tier I to other Tier I
features, your Tier II to other Tier II features, and your
Tier III to other Tier III capstones. This will help you
remain close to the expected power output of Legendary
Characters!

Path Examples
Pure Pathing. A 25th-level Sorcerer has elected to follow the
Path of the Primordial, spending each Heroic Path
Advancement in their chosen path and reaching the
Primordial Tier III Heroic Feat.

Multi-Pathing. A 24th-level Fighter has advanced the Warlord
Heroic Path until the Tier II Combat Master Heroic Feat and
has one step left. Knowing one of their main weaknesses is
their lack of mobility, they choose to initiate the Limitless
Path, granting them the Lightness Tier I Heroic Feat.

Cross-Pathing. A 23rd-level Barbarian took their first step in the
Titan path, gaining the Overwhelming Might Tier I Heroic
Feat, before choosing to pivot into the Unbreakable Heroic
Path with their next step, gaining the Iron Stance Heroic Perk.
Then, following the progression of the Unbreakable Heroic
Path, they gain the Untarnished Blood Tier II Heroic Feat at
level 23.

No Pathing. A 23rd-level Paladin has chosen to start three
separate Heroic Paths–Nightingale, Chosen One, and
Unbreakable–gaining the Spellblade, Fated, and Greater
Toughness feats, all Tier I. No steps beyond the first in any of
them!

Original PDF page 86

Heroic Paths

The following is a list of all Heroic Paths available as of
this Alpha release. Each Heroic Path is named after its
Tier III Heroic Feat, but, as mentioned before, can
branch into other paths. Take the time to evaluate all
your options when you level up!

When you gain a Heroic Perk, you can use it as an
entry point into a connected Heroic Path, provided you

Heroic Paths

Name
Prerequisites
Connected Paths

Arcane Marksman
Spellcasting or Pact Magic
Archmagus, Legendary Psion

Archmagus
Spellcasting or Pact Magic
Arcane Marksman, Third Eye

Chosen One
None
None

Herald
Bard, Cleric, Paladin, or Warlock
Sovereign, Overlord

Hierophant
Bard, Cleric, or Druid
Herald, Sovereign, Third Eye

Legendary Psion
Intelligence 20+
Archmagus, Arcane Marksman

Legendary Sniper
Dexterity 20+
Warlord

Limitless
Dexterity 20+
Warlord

Nightingale
Spellcasting or Pact Magic
Arcane Marksman, Shadowblade

Overlord
Spellcasting or Pact Magic
Sovereign

Primordial
Spellcasting or Pact Magic
None

Runeblood
Barbarian, Druid, or Ranger
Unbreakable

Shadowblade
Dexterity 20+
Legendary Sniper, Warlord

Sovereign
Bard, Sorcerer, or Warlock
Overlord

Third Eye
Wisdom 20+
Archmagus, Herald

Titan
Strength 20+
Unbreakable, Warlord

Unbreakable
Barbarian, Fighter, Paladin, or Monk
Titan

Warlord
Strength or Dexterity 20+
None

meet its prerequisites at the time of entry. Even if you’ve
stepped into a Heroic Perk that connects to other
Heroic Paths, you won’t always be able to branch into
them–you must meet the destination path’s
prerequisites. This may motivate you to use your
Legendary Score Improvements in a certain way! Pay
attention not only to the pathing, but also to the
prerequisites you’ll need to meet to actually bear out
your plan.

Original PDF page 87

Heroic Path: Arcane Marksman

Prerequisite: Spellcasting or Pact Magic
Tier I: Fingers of Toth

Arcane Precision. You have a +3 bonus to spell attack
rolls. Additionally, your critical range for spell attacks is
increased by 1.

Quickened Cantrip. When you cast a spell of 1st level
or higher, you can cast a cantrip with a casting time of 1
action or 1 bonus action as part of the same action used
to cast the leveled spell.

Heroic Perk: Magical Geometry

Prerequisite: Fingers of Toth

Your critical range for spell attacks is increased by 1.
When you score a critical hit with a spell attack, the
target cannot take reactions until the start of its next
turn, as the precision of the strike disrupts its ability to
respond.
Tier II: Sharpened Magic

Prerequisite: Magical Geometry

Arcane Damage. Whenever you deal damage with a
spell of 5th-level or lower, you can choose to change the
damage type of the spell to force damage for the
purpose of overcoming resistances.

Burst of Magic. When you deal damage with a spell,
you can choose to deal maximum damage with that
spell. Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again
until you finish a long rest.
Heroic Perk: Arcane Shield

Prerequisite: Sharpened Magic

When you deal force damage, you can expend a 5thlevel spell slot to gain an amount of temporary hit points
equal to half the amount of force damage dealt.
Tier III: Arcane Marksman

Prerequisite: Sharpened Magic, Arcane Shield

Seeking Magic. Your spell attacks cannot be made at
disadvantage. When you cast a spell that requires a
spell attack roll, you can choose to treat the d20 roll as a
15 before adding your attack bonus. Once you use this
feature, you cannot do so again until the start of your
next turn. In addition, your critical range for spell
attacks is increased by 1.

Arcane Damage Mastery. When you deal force
damage, you can expend a spell slot to deal an
additional amount of force damage equal to the spell
slot expended x 5. You can now change the damage type
of all your spells to force damage, regardless of their
level.

Path Connections
Archmagus. Instead of the Magical Geometry Heroic Perk,
you can take the Erudition Perk, changing to the Archmagus
path.

Legendary Psion. Instead of the Magical Geometry Heroic
Perk, you can take the Mind Tricks Perk, changing to the
Legendary Psion path.

Original PDF page 88

Heroic Path: Archmagus

Prerequisites: Spellcasting or Pact Magic.
Tier I: Arcane Savant

Spell Resistance. You have resistance to damage from
spells.

Greater Magic. Your spell save DC is increased by 1.

Heroic Perk: Erudition

Prerequisite: Arcane Savant

You automatically identify the school, casting time,
maximum duration and general effects of spells being
cast within 60 feet of you, or ongoing spells whose
effects you can sense. When you are forced to make a
saving throw against a spell you’ve identified in this
way, you can use your reaction to gain advantage on the
roll. Additionally, you learn 3 spells of up to 7th level
from any spellcasting list. They count as spells of your
class for you.

Tier II: Expanded Magic

Prerequisite: Erudition

Stalwart Concentration. When you roll a Constitution
saving throw to maintain concentration on spells, the
DC cannot exceed 30. You add your spellcasting ability
modifier to saving throws made to maintain
concentration on spells.

Unendurable Magic. Your spell save DC is increased
by 1.

Heroic Perk: Arcane Mastery

Prerequisite: Expanded Magic

When you cast a spell, that spell cannot be nullified by
magic of 7th level or lower.

Tier III: Archmagus

Prerequisite: Arcane Mastery

Ascended Magic. Your spell save DC is increased by 2.

Inexhaustible Conduits. You can use Legendary
Points to cast lesser spells. You can expend 1
Legendary Point to cast a spell up to 3rd level and 2
Legendary Points to cast a spell up to 7th level.

Path Connections
Arcane Marksman. Instead of the Erudition Heroic Perk, you
can take the Magical Geometry Perk, changing to the Arcane
Marksman path.

Third Eye. Instead of the Arcane Mastery Heroic Perk, you
can take the Ascended Mind Heroic Perk, changing to the
Third Eye path.

Original PDF page 89

Heroic Path: Chosen One

No prerequisites
Tier I: Fated

Spared by Destiny. When you take damage, you can
use your reaction to ignore that instance of damage, as
fate inexplicably intervenes, reserving you for a higher
purpose. Once you use this feature, you cannot use it
again until you finish a long rest.

Strings of Fate. When a creature within 60 feet of you
makes an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw, you
can use your reaction to influence the outcome. Roll
1d6 and apply the result as a bonus or penalty to the
roll. You can choose to do so after the roll is made but
before any of its results are announced.
Heroic Perk: Convergence

Prerequisite: Fated

Fate bends the world towards you. As an action, you can
declare a Convergence, a particular event that will
happen in the next hour. This can be a general
declaration, such as obtaining an item, reaching a
location, or defeating a Legendary Foe–but it can also
be a specific, immediate need, such as having the
strength to jump over a chasm. While your Convergence
is active, you can choose to give yourself, or a creature
within 60 feet of you, advantage on an ability check,
attack roll, or saving throw related to the Convergence
you declared. Once you do so, the Convergence ends.
You can only have one Convergence declared at a time.
Tier II: Destined

Prerequisite: Fated, Convergence

Written in the Stars. Increase an ability score of your
choice by 2. Your maximum for that score is increased
by 2 as well.

Fated Criticality. When you make an attack against a
target within range, you can turn a miss or regular hit
into a critical hit. Alternatively, when you make an
ability check or saving throw, you can treat the d20 roll
as a 20. You can choose to do so after rolling but before
any of the roll’s effects are announced. Once you use
this ability, you must finish a long rest before you can do
so again.
Heroic Perk: Threads Unraveled

Prerequisite: Fated, Destined

Your connection to fate extends to those around you.
When you use your Spared by Destiny ability, you can
choose to extend the benefit to another creature within
60 feet that is taking the same instance of damage, as
with an area of effect spell such as Fireball or Meteor
Swarm. In addition, you now roll 1d8 instead of 1d6
when using your Strings of Fate feature.

Tier III: Chosen One

Prerequisite: Threads Unraveled, Destined

Another Chance. When an effect would kill you
outright, or damage would reduce you to 0 hit points,
you drop to 1 hit point instead. Once this feature
triggers, it cannot trigger again for a minute.

Thrice Fated. You can use your Fated Criticality three
times between long rests.

Original PDF page 90

Heroic Path: Herald

Prerequisites: Bard, Cleric, Paladin, or Warlock
Tier I: Voice

Irrepressible Voice. You can choose to make your
voice be heard up to 300 feet, and it can be understood
regardless of language. You cannot be magically
silenced or otherwise prevented from speaking unless
you are paralyzed, stunned, or unconscious. Magic
cannot compel your speech in any way.

Proclamation. As an action, you speak a proclamation
tied to your deity. You can also come up with your own
proclamation, with a similar effect, at the DM’s
discretion.

+

Allies who can hear you gain temporary hit points
equal to your level and advantage on their next
attack roll or saving throw.

+

Creatures of your choice that can hear you must
succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your
spell save DC or be affected by the Command spell.

An ongoing 5th-level or lower spell or magical effect
of your choice within 30 feet of you ends.
+

Heroic Perk: Divine Arrival

Prerequisite: Voice

You can choose to make yourself known whenever you
enter a settlement, battlefield, or planar domain. The
range at which you can announce yourself is an amount
of miles equal to your total character level. Creatures
instinctively know something important and divine has
arrived. You can force creatures of CR 5 or lower to flee,
gather near your position, or pray at your coming. The
DM determines exactly how creatures respond to your
arrival based on their nature, disposition and your
intent.
Tier II: Omen Incarnate

Prerequisite: Divine Arrival

Power of the Voice. Your spell save DC is increased
by 2 for spells that have verbal components.

Edict. As an action, you can speak a single sentence
aloud, dictating a new rule of creation. Choose one of
the following effects that applies in a 60-foot radius
centered on yourself for the next minute. You can also
come up with your own edict, with a similarly powerful
effect, at the DM’s discretion. Once you use this ability,
you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

Edict of Bane: Creatures of your choice have
disadvantage on all saving throws.
+

+

Edict of Sanctuary: Allies gain resistance to all
damage. They gain immunity to damage types they
were already resistant to.

+

Edict of Forbiddance: A specific course of action
(fleeing, lying, attacking a specific creature) cannot
be performed. If a creature attempts to take the
action regardless, it must succeed on a Wisdom
saving throw against your spell save DC or take
10d6 psychic damage and be unable to take their
desired action. Creatures that succeed on this saving
throw become immune to your Edict of Forbiddance
for the next 24 hours.

Heroic Perk: Words Beyond Worlds

Prerequisite: Omen Incarnate

You ignore material and somatic components when
casting spells. As a bonus action, you can send a mental
message to a creature of your choice, regardless of
distance and even across planes of existence. The
creature hears a message of your choice in its mind,
recognizes you as the sender if it knows you, and can
answer in a like manner immediately if you choose to
allow it. This feature enables creatures with Intelligence
scores of at least 3 to understand the meaning of your
message. Creatures that don’t consider you an enemy
are motivated to heed your message and its intent–
warnings will be taken seriously, and requests will be
acquiesced unless exceedingly difficult or costly to their
recipient.
Tier III: Herald

Prerequisite: Words Beyond Worlds

Immortal Echo. You are immune to the frightened and
charmed conditions. If you fall unconscious or die but
your body is not destroyed, your voice continues to echo,
allowing you to speak and cast spells as if you were
conscious until the end of your next turn. If you are
dying, this effect also stabilizes you at the end of that
turn.

Word of Creation. As an action, you speak a word of
creation, replicating the effects of any spell of 9th level
or lower that you are familiar with. Once you use this
ability, you cannot do so again until you finish a long
rest.

Path Connections

Sovereign. Instead of the Divine Arrival Heroic Perk, you can
take the Greater Charms Perk, changing to the Sovereign
path.

Overlord. Instead of the Divine Arrival Heroic Perk, you can
take the Thrall Perk, changing to the Overlord path.

Original PDF page 91

Heroic Path: Hierophant

Prerequisite: Bard, Cleric, or Druid
Tier I: Exalted Presence

Amplified Boons. When you use a class feature that
targets willing creatures, such as Bardic Inspiration or
Channel Divinity, you can target twice the number of
creatures the feature normally allows. All creatures
must be within the feature’s range.

Spell Holding. When you take the Ready action to
prepare a spell, you may hold that spell for up to 1
minute without releasing it, maintaining concentration
as normal. The spell slot is not expended unless the
trigger occurs and the spell is successfully cast. If your
concentration ends before the trigger occurs, the spell’s
energy dissipates back into your conduits without
expending the slot.
Heroic Perk: Hierophant’s
Sanctuary

Prerequisite: Exalted Presence

You emit a permanent aura of protection in a 30-foot
radius sphere centered on you. Friendly creatures
within the area have a bonus to their saving throws
equal to half your proficiency bonus (rounded down).
When a friendly creature within the area is reduced to 0
hit points but not killed outright, you can use your
reaction to have them be reduced to 1 hit point instead.
Tier II: Perfected Boons

Prerequisite: Hierophant’s Sanctuary

Transcendent Healing. When you cast a spell that
restores hit points, each target regains additional hit
points equal to your spellcasting ability modifier + your
proficiency bonus. Healing spells you cast also remove
one condition of your choice from each target among
the following: blinded, charmed, deafened, frightened,
or poisoned.

Delegated Concentration. When you cast a
concentration spell that targets or benefits a willing
creature, that creature can maintain concentration on
the spell on your behalf. If their concentration is broken,
the spell ends. If the creature takes damage, they roll
the Constitution saving throw to maintain concentration
using their own statistics.

Amplified Magic. When you cast a spell that targets
willing creatures, you can target twice the number of
creatures the spell normally allows. All creatures must
be within the spell’s range.
Heroic Perk: Chooser of Conduits

Prerequisite: Exalted Presence, Perfected Boons

Spells you hold with your Spell Holding ability can be
released by your allies. When you have a spell held, you
can designate a willing creature you can touch as its

conduit. The conduit begins holding the spell in your
stead. You lose the ability to release the spell with your
designated trigger. The conduit can release the held
spell using their action on their turn, originating from
them but using your spell save DC and spell attack
bonus. If the spell requires concentration, the conduit is
the one that must maintain it.
Tier III: Hierophant

Prerequisite: Exalted Presence, Hierophant’s
Sanctuary, Perfected Boons, Chooser of Conduits

Great Guide. Your Amplified Boons and Amplified
Magic features now allow you to target three times the
number of creatures a spell or feature normally allows.
The range of your Hierophant’s Sanctuary aura expands
to 60 feet.

Word of Creation. As an action, you speak a word of
creation, replicating the effects of any spell of 9th level
or lower that you are familiar with. Once you use this
ability, you cannot do so again until you finish a long
rest.

Path Connections
Herald. Instead of the Hierophant’s Sanctuary Heroic Perk,
you can take the Divine Arrival Perk, changing to the Herald
path.

Sovereign. Instead of the Hierophant’s Sanctuary Heroic
Perk, you can take the Greater Charms Perk, changing to the
Sovereign path.

Third Eye. Instead of the Chooser of Conduits Heroic Perk,
you can take the Ascended Mind Perk, changing to the Third
Eye path.

Original PDF page 92

Heroic Path: Legendary Psion

Prerequisite: An Intelligence score of 20 or higher
Tier I: Awakened Mind

Expanded Intellect. Your Intelligence score increases
by 2, as does your Intelligence score maximum.

Psychic Deflection. When you take psychic damage,
you can use your reaction to nullify it. Optionally, you
can redirect it toward a creature you can see within 60
feet. It must succeed on an Intelligence saving throw
against a DC equal to 8 + your Intelligence modifier +
your proficiency bonus or take the full instance damage
in your stead.

Telekinetic. You are always under the effects of the
Telekinesis spell. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability
for resolving contests against creatures you target with
your telekinetic grip. The range of your telekinesis is
120 feet rather than 60 feet, and you can target up to
two creatures or objects simultaneously.
Heroic Perk: Mind Tricks

Prerequisite: Awakened Mind

You can cast the Suggestion spell at will without
expending a spell slot. You cannot be affected by spells
or magical effects that would sense your emotions or
read your thoughts. When a creature attempts to read
your thoughts or emotions, you can present false
thoughts or feelings. The mind reader can notice their
falseness by succeeding on a DC 30 Wisdom (Insight)
check.
Tier II: Telekinetic Master

Prerequisite: Mind Tricks

Ascended Intellect. Your Intelligence score increases
by 2, as does your Intelligence score maximum.

Mind Fortress. Your mind becomes an impenetrable
fortress. You are immune to any effect that would
charm, frighten, or make you act against your will.
When a creature targets you with such an effect, it must
succeed on an Intelligence saving throw against a DC
equal to 8 + your Intelligence modifier + your
proficiency bonus or take 10d8 psychic damage as the
attempt rebounds.

Psionic Field. You can add your Intelligence modifier
to your AC calculations. When a creature within 30 feet
of you makes an attack, you can use your reaction to sap
their focus, giving them disadvantage on the attack.
When a projectile of Small size or smaller passes within
30 feet of you, you can use your reaction to stop the
projectile, causing it to fall to the ground.
Heroic Perk: Psionic Hub

Prerequisite: Telekinetic Master

While you are conscious, allies of your choice within
600 feet share a telepathic link. Linked creatures can
communicate silently and freely, cannot be surprised
while conscious, and have advantage on Intelligence,
Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws.
Tier III: Legendary Psion

Prerequisite: Psionic Hub

Psionic Supremacy. You and allies of your choice
within 30 feet of you are immune to psychic damage.

Greater Psionic Field. Your mind radiates a
permanent Psionic Field in a 30-foot radius centered on
you. All creatures of your choice within your Psionic
Field can add your Intelligence modifier to their AC
calculations. As a bonus action, you can send a psychic
blast to a creature within the Psionic Field. That
creature must succeed on an Intelligence saving throw
against a DC equal to 8 + your Intelligence modifier +
your proficiency bonus or take 8d8 psychic damage and
be stunned until the end of their next turn.

Path Connections
Archmagus. Instead of the Mind Tricks Heroic Perk, you can
take the Erudition Perk, changing to the Archmagus path.

Arcane Marksman. Instead of the Mind Tricks Heroic Perk,
you can take the Magical Geometry Perk, changing to the
Arcane Marksman path.

Original PDF page 93

Heroic Path: Legendary Sniper

Prerequisite: A Dexterity score of 20 or higher
Tier I: Heroic Sharpshooter

Sniper’s Range. While using a ranged weapon, you
double its normal and long range. Your ranged weapon
attacks ignore half and three-quarters cover.

Pinning Shot. Once per round, when you hit a Huge or
smaller creature with a ranged weapon attack, you can
choose to pin them. The creature’s speed is reduced by
20 feet until the end of its next turn.

Greater Accuracy. Your ranged weapon attacks
cannot be made at disadvantage, and your critical range
is increased by 1 for attacks made with ranged
weapons.
Heroic Perk: Hunter’s Eye

Prerequisite: Heroic Sharpshooter

You have a +5 bonus to your Wisdom (Perception)
checks and Initiative rolls. You can see in lightly
obscured areas, such as those created by fog, heavy rain
and dim light as if they were clear.
Tier II: Absolute Precision

Prerequisite: Hunter’s Eye

Penetrating Shots. Your ranged weapon attacks
ignore resistance to piercing damage.

Legendary Shot (Multiplier Attack). You can use
your action to perform a Legendary Shot. Make a single
ranged weapon attack against a creature within range.
On a hit, the target takes 4d8 x 5 of the weapon’s
damage type. This attack does not benefit from any
bonus to its damage. On a critical hit, you do not
multiply the damage dice by 2, but instead deal 6d8 x 5
weapon damage. This Multiplier Attack does not expend
Legendary Points when used.
Heroic Perk: Armor Piercer

Prerequisite: Absolute Precision

Once per round, when you hit a creature with a ranged
weapon attack, you can choose to expose a weak point
in their defenses. The creature’s AC is reduced by 5
until the end of your next turn.

Tier III: Legendary Sniper

Prerequisite: Armor Piercer, Absolute Precision

Unfaltering Pulse. When you make a ranged weapon
attack, you can choose to treat the d20 roll as a 15
before adding your attack bonus.

Double Shot. Once per round, when you make a
ranged weapon attack, you can expend 1 LP to make
two identical attacks instead of one. You can choose to
target the same creature twice or target different

creatures within 60 feet of each other. This feature can
be used on your Legendary Shot ability and Mythical
Techniques performed by making a ranged weapon
attack.

Path Connections
Warlord. Instead of the Hunter’s Eye Heroic Perk, you can
take the Defensive Proficiency Perk, changing to the Warlord
path.

Original PDF page 94

Heroic Path: Limitless

Prerequisite: A Dexterity Score of 20 or higher
Tier I: Lightness

Unbridled Step. You gain a fly speed equal to your
walking speed, as you become capable of running on
any surface, including air. You cannot take damage as a
result of falling unless you are restrained or
incapacitated.

Unpinnable. You have advantage on saving throws and
ability checks against being grappled or restrained.
Heroic Perk: Greater Speed

Prerequisite: Lightness

Your maximum walking speed is doubled. Whenever
you take the Dash action, your AC is increased by 2 until
the start of your next turn.
Tier II: Celerity

Prerequisite: Greater Speed

Phasing Step. You can move through Medium or
smaller objects and creatures as if they were difficult
terrain. If you end your turn inside an object or creature,
you are pushed to the nearest unoccupied space and
take 20 force damage.

Beyond Restraint. You cannot be grappled,
restrained, shoved, or knocked prone unless you are
incapacitated.

Opening Act. When you roll initiative, you can choose
to ignore the result of the roll and act on initiative count
30 (winning ties) instead.
Heroic Perk: Unstoppable Movement

Prerequisite: Celerity

You don’t provoke opportunity attacks when moving in
and out of an enemy’s range. Whenever you take the
Disengage action, your AC is increased by 3 until the
start of your next turn. Creatures you are carrying
benefit from this AC increase.

Tier III: Limitless

Prerequisite: Unstoppable Movement

Extra Turn. At Initiative count 10, you can take an
extra turn. Your extra turn has the following limitations:
you can use either a bonus action or an action but not
both, your speed is halved, you cannot use the Recover
action, and you cannot use abilities that consume 2 or
more Legendary Points.

Flash Speed. You can choose to spend your entire turn
moving. When you do so, you spend your action, bonus
action, and movement on your turn to gain a special
Flash Speed equal to 10 times your walking speed. You
can continue using this speed as long as you take no

other action other than moving on your turn. You can
still use reactions while using your Flash Speed.

Path Connections
Warlord. Instead of the Unstoppable Movement Heroic Perk,
you can take the Perfect Body Perk, changing to the Warlord
path.

Original PDF page 95

Heroic Path: Nightingale

Prerequisite: Spellcasting or Pact Magic
Tier I: Spellblade

Arcane Edge. When you hit a creature with a melee
weapon attack, you can channel magical energy through
the blade. The attack deals force damage instead of its
usual damage type, and the creature has disadvantage
on its first saving throw against a spell you cast before
the end of your next turn.

Spell & Blade. When you cast a spell, you can make
one melee weapon attack against a creature within your
melee range as a bonus action.

Spellstrike. When you cast a spell of 5th level or lower
that requires a spell attack roll, you can deliver it
through a melee weapon attack instead. Make one
melee weapon attack roll. On a hit, the attack deals its
normal damage, and the spell takes effect without
requiring a separate attack roll. You can only use
Spellstrike once per round.
Heroic Perk: Spellblade’s Retort

Prerequisite: Spellblade

Your movement does not provoke opportunity attacks.
When a creature misses you with a melee attack, you
can use your reaction to immediately make one melee
weapon attack or cast a spell of 5th level or lower that
targets only that creature.
Tier II: Arcane Duelist

Prerequisite: Spellblade’s Retort

Spellblade Mastery. You can deliver any spell of 5th
level or lower that has a casting time of one action or
one bonus action through your Spellstrike, not just
spells that would require an attack roll. When you do so,
the spell only affects the target you hit, even if it is an
area spell or a spell that targets multiple creatures.
Spells delivered through Spellstrike that would
normally require a saving throw instead take full effect
automatically on a hit. Subsequent saving throws
beyond the first one, such as those required by the
Flesh to Stone spell, are rolled as normal. A creature
that possesses Legendary Points or Legendary
Resistances can expend 1 Legendary Point to avoid
being affected by a spell delivered through Spellstrike.

Parrying Cast. When you take the Dodge action, you
can cast a spell of 5th level or lower as part of that same
action.
Heroic Perk: Lethal Cadence

Prerequisite: Arcane Duelist

Your Dexterity score increases by 2, as does your
maximum for that score. When you use your Spellstrike
feature, you can make an additional attack as part of the

Cast a Spell action. In addition, you can take the Dodge
action as a bonus action on your turn.
Tier III: Nightingale

Prerequisite: Arcane Duelist, Lethal Cadence

Nightingale’s Prowess. You can deliver spells of up to
7th level through your Spellstrike that have a casting
time of one action or bonus action.

Arcane Arsenal. Whenever you use your Spellstrike
feature, a phantom duplicate of the weapon you used for
the attack materializes and orbits you. You can have a
maximum of 6 phantom weapons active simultaneously.
Each phantom weapon lasts for 1 minute or until
dismissed.

You can use your phantom weapons in two ways:

+

Launch: As a bonus action, you launch a number of
phantom weapons of your choice at a target within
60 feet. Each weapon makes a separate attack using
the same attack bonus and damage profile as the
weapon that created it. A phantom weapon deals
force damage instead of its normal type. After
making the attack, the phantom weapon is
consumed and disappears.

+

Spellforge: As a bonus action, you consume any
number of phantom weapons to cast a spell without
expending a spell slot. The level of the spell you can
cast equals the number of phantom weapons
consumed–consuming 3 phantom weapons casts a
spell of 3rd level, consuming 5 phantom weapons
casts a spell of 5th level, and so on. You must know
or have prepared the spell you wish to cast, and you
must have the required components.

Path Connections

Arcane Marksman. Instead of the Spellblade’s Retort Heroic
Perk, you can take the Magical Geometry Perk, changing to
the Arcane Marksman path.

Shadowblade. Instead of the Spellblade’s Retort Heroic Perk,
you can take the Death Mark Perk, changing to the
Shadowblade path.

Original PDF page 96

Heroic Path: Overlord

Prerequisite: Spellcasting or Pact Magic
Tier I: Dread Master

Dread Aura. You radiate an aura of oppressive
darkness in a 30-foot radius. Creatures of your choice
that start their turn within the aura must succeed on a
Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or be
frightened of you for one minute. An affected creature
can repeat the saving throw at the start of each of its
turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. A creature
that succeeds on this saving throw is immune to your
Dread Aura for the next 24 hours.

Dark Authority. You can use an action to attempt to
subdue a creature you can see within 60 feet. The
creature must succeed on a Charisma saving throw
against your spell save DC or be compelled to do your
bidding for 1 minute, as if under the effects of the
Dominate Monster spell. While under this effect, the
creature is aware it is being controlled but cannot resist.
A creature that is frightened of you automatically fails
this saving throw. Creatures immune to the frightened
condition are not affected by this feature. You can only
have one creature controlled through this feature at a
time.
Heroic Perk: Thrall

Prerequisite: Dread Master

When a creature dies within 60 feet of you, you can use
your reaction to raise it as a thrall under your command.
A thrall uses the creature’s original stat block, but its
maximum hit points are halved, and it cannot use
legendary actions, legendary resistances, or legendary
points .

You can have an unlimited number of thralls of CR 5
or lower active simultaneously. You can only maintain
one thrall of CR 6 or higher at a time–raising a new one
immediately dismisses the previous. Creatures of CR 25
or higher cannot be raised as thralls.

Thralls act on your initiative count immediately after
you and obey your commands without question. A thrall
crumbles to dust when it is reduced to 0 hit points or
after 24 hours. You can also dismiss a thrall as a free
action.
Tier II: Dark Tyrant

Prerequisite: Thrall

Crushing Will. Your spell save DC is increased by 2.
When a creature succeeds on a saving throw against
one of your spells, it takes psychic damage equal to your
spellcasting ability modifier + your proficiency bonus as
your dark will lashes out against its defiance. A creature
can only be affected by this feature once per turn.

Edict of Domination. When you cast a spell that
charms or frightens a creature, you can choose to make
the effect permanent until dispelled, rather than using

the spell’s normal duration. If you choose to do so and
the spell’s full normal duration elapses, you are no
longer required to maintain concentration on it to
extend its effects. If your concentration ends before the
full duration elapses, the effects end as normal.

Heroic Perk: Soul Harvest

Prerequisite: Thrall, Dark Tyrant

When a creature of CR 25 or lower dies within 60 feet
of you, you can use your reaction to trap its soul within a
container of your choosing, preventing it from passing
onto the afterlife. You can hold a number of souls equal
to your spellcasting ability modifier simultaneously.

At any time, you can release a trapped soul to raise it
as a Thrall, manifesting it in a spectral body. Thralls
raised this way are immune to all nonmagical damage.
When a spectral Thrall is destroyed within 60 feet of
you, you can use your reaction to recapture its soul,
returning it to your container and allowing you to raise
it again later.

Soul Harvest also improves your existing Thrall
capabilities in the following ways: you can have one
additional Thrall of CR 6 or higher active
simultaneously, and all your Thralls last for a maximum
of 1 week instead of 24 hours.
Tier III: Overlord

Prerequisite: Thrall, Soul Harvest

Enfeebling Terror. Creatures that are frightened by
you are also Impaired for the duration.

Apotheosis. Once per long rest, as an action, you
undergo a terrifying transformation, channeling the full
depth of your dark power. For 1 minute, you gain the
following benefits:

Your spells and class features ignore immunity to
the frightened condition for the duration.
+

When you cast a spell that deals damage, it deals
maximum damage instead of rolling.
+

+

When a creature dies within 120 feet of you, you can
choose to automatically raise it as a thrall without
requiring a reaction, ignoring your normal thrall
limit for the duration. Thralls raised in this manner
crumble to dust when your Apotheosis ends.

Path Connections
Sovereign. Instead of the Thrall Heroic Perk, you can take the
Greater Charms Perk, changing to the Sovereign path.

Original PDF page 97

Heroic Path: Primordial

Prerequisite: Spellcasting or Pact Magic
Tier I: Elemental Awakening

Primordial Element. When you gain this Heroic Feat,
choose one of the following damage types: acid, cold, fire,
lightning, or thunder. This is your Primordial Element.

Elemental Attunement. You gain immunity to your
Primordial Element. Your spells and class features that
deal damage of your Primordial Element ignore
resistance, and treat immunity as if it were resistance.
Heroic Perk: Elemental Mastery

Prerequisite: Elemental Awakening

You can replace the damage type of any spell you cast
with your Primordial Element. When you deal damage of
your Primordial Element, the spell gains one of the
following modifiers, depending on your element, applied
to any creature that fails its saving throw or is hit by the
spell. A creature can only be affected once per round by
one of these effects.

Acid: The creature’s AC is reduced by 3 until the start of
your next turn.

Cold: The creature’s speed is reduced by 15 feet until the
end of its next turn.

Fire: The creature takes an additional 3d10 fire damage
at the start of its next turn.

Lightning: The creature cannot take reactions until the
end of its next turn.

Thunder: The creature must succeed on a Strength saving
throw against your spell save DC or be pushed up to 30
feet away from you and knocked prone.
Tier II: Conduit

Prerequisite: Elemental Mastery

Primordial Absorption. When you take damage of your
Primordial Element, you regain an amount of hit points
equal to half the damage dealt, up to a maximum of 100
hit points regained in this way between long rests.

Prana Overchanneling. When you deal damage of your
Primordial Element, you can expend a spell slot of 5th
level or higher to deal maximum damage instead of
rolling.
Heroic Perk: Form of the Elements

Prerequisite: Conduit

You gain a fly speed of 60 feet, propelled by your element–
jets of flame, arcs of lightning, a howling wind, or a
torrent of acid. While flying, you emit your Primordial
Element in a 15-foot radius centered on you. Creatures of
your choice within this area take 3d8 of your Primordial
Element’s damage type at the start of your turn.

Tier III: Primordial

Prerequisite: Form of the Elements

Ancient Power. Your spellcasting ability score increases
by 2, as does your maximum for that score. The damage
dice of all spells you cast that deal damage of your
Primordial Element are increased by one die category, up
to d12s–d4s becomes d6s, d6s becomes d8s, d8s
becomes d10s, and d10s becomes d12s.

Cataclysm. As an action on your turn, you can unleash
the full, uncontrolled fury of your element. :Acid:
Corrosive Deluge: A torrent of acid falls in a 60-foot radius
centered on a point you can see within 120 feet. Creatures
in that area must make a Dexterity saving throw against
your spell save DC, taking 15d8 acid damage and having
their AC reduced by 3 until they finish a long rest, or
taking half damage and suffering no AC reduction on a
success.

Cold: Absolute Zero: A wave of killing frost erupts from
you in a 60-foot radius sphere. Creatures in that area
must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell
save DC, taking 10d8 cold damage and being petrified
until the end of their next turn on a failed save, or taking
only half damage and having their maximum speed halved
until the end of their next turn on a success.

Fire: Conflagration: A pillar of elemental fire descends in
a 30-foot radius, 120-foot high cylinder centered on a
point you can see within 120 feet. Creatures in that area
must make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell
save DC. On a failed save, they take 15d8 fire damage and
are set burning, taking an additional 5d8 fire damage at
the start of each of their turns for 1 minute or until they
douse the flames by being submerged in water or
spending an action rolling on the ground. On a success,
they take half the initial damage and do not start burning.

Lightning: Stunning Storm: Lightning erupts from you,
arching between each creature of your choice you can see
within 120 feet. Each target must make a Dexterity saving
throw against your spell save DC, taking 10d8 lightning
damage and being stunned until the end of their next turn
on a failed save, or taking half as much damage and not
being stunned on a success.

Thunder: World Shatter: A concussive wave of thunder
tears outward from you in a 90-foot cone in front of you.
Creatures in that area must make a Constitution saving
throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, they
take 15d8 thunder damage, are knocked prone, and are
deafened for the next 24 hours. On a successful save, they
take only half damage, aren’t knocked prone, and are
deafened until the end of their next turn. Structures and
objects in the area take maximum damage.

Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again until
you finish a long rest.

Original PDF page 98

Heroic Path: Runeblood

Prerequisite: Barbarian, Druid, or Ranger
Tier I: Living Rune

Runic Body. Ancient runes of power have drawn
themselves across your skin, manifested by the primal
power flowing through you. You gain resistance to one of
the following damage types: acid, cold, fire, lightning,
necrotic, poison, radiant, or thunder. These resistances
apply to alternative forms, such as the ones created by the
Wild Shape class feature. Additionally, your unarmed
strikes and natural weapon attacks become magical.

Runic Boon. You gain one of the following runic boons,
which is always active.

Rune of the Heart: If you start your turn with less than
half your total hit points, you regain 10 hit points.
+

Rune of the Wind: You gain a fly speed of 50 feet.
+

+

Rune of the Deep: You gain a swim speed equal to your
walking speed, can breathe underwater, and have
resistance to cold damage.

+

Rune of the Earth: You cannot be knocked prone or
moved against your will. You gain resistance to
bludgeoning damage.

Rune of the Flame: You become immune to fire damage.
+

Rune of the Dead: You become immune to necrotic
damage.
+

+

Rune of the Void: You gain darkvision out to a range of
120 feet. This darkvision is not impeded by magical
darkness. The first time you damage a creature in a
round of combat, it must succeed on a DC 25
Constitution saving throw or be unable to regain hit
points until the start of your next turn.

Heroic Perk: Runic Awakening

Prerequisite: Living Rune

Your runes expand across your body, granting you access to
an additional Runic Boon from your Living Rune Heroic
Feat.

Tier II: Runic Conduit

Prerequisite: Living Rune, Runic Awakening

Ancient Warding. The runes etched into your skin protect
you from the forces that shaped them. When a creature
targets you with a spell of 5th level or lower, you can use
your reaction to absorb it, nullifying its effects for you. Once
you absorb a spell in this way, you cannot do so again until
the start of your next turn.

Runic Sense. Your runes allow you to sense the magical
properties of nearby effects and objects as if you were
always under the Detect Magic spell.

Runic Boon. You gain an additional Runic Boon from
your Living Rune feat.

Heroic Perk: Greater Runes

Prerequisite: Runic Conduit

Your chosen runes from your Living Rune feat gain
improved effects, replacing the previous one.

Greater Rune of the Heart: You regain 10 hit points at
the start of each of your turns.
+

Greater Rune of the Wind: You gain a fly speed of 100
feet.
+

+

Greater Rune of the Deep: You gain a swim speed
equal to thrice your walking speed, can breathe
underwater, and have immunity to cold damage.

+

Greater Rune of the Earth: You cannot be knocked
prone, shoved or moved. You gain resistance to
bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

+

Greater Rune of the Flame: You become immune to
fire damage. When you take fire damage, you regain
an amount of hit points equal to half the fire damage
taken.

+

Greater Rune of the Dead: You become immune to
necrotic damage and resistant to dark damage. When
you take necrotic damage, you regain an amount of hit
points equal to half the necrotic damage taken.

+

Greater Rune of the Void: You gain darkvision out to a
range of 180 feet. This darkvision is not impeded by
magical darkness and you can perceive color
irrespective of lighting conditions. The first time you
damage a creature in a round of combat, it must
succeed on a DC 30 Constitution saving throw or be
unable to regain hit points until the start of your next
turn.

Tier III: Runeblood

Prerequisite: Greater Runes

World Rune. You gain access to two additional Greater
Runic Boons from your Greater Runes heroic perk, for a
total of 5.

Limited Magic Immunity. You cannot be affected or
detected by spells and magical effects of 7th-level or lower
unless you wish to be.

Path Connections
Unbreakable. Instead of the Greater Runes Heroic Perk, you
can take the Stone Body Perk, changing to the Unbreakable
path.

Original PDF page 99

Heroic Path: Shadowblade

Prerequisite: Dexterity Score of 20 or higher
Tier I: Phantom Strike

Shadow Step. As a bonus action, you can teleport up
to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see,
alongside anything you are wearing or carrying. You
have advantage on the first attack you make before the
end of your turn after using this feature.

Unseen Threat. You can use the Hide action as a
bonus action. When you are hidden from a creature and
hit it with an attack, it cannot use reactions until the end
of its next turn.

Of Darkness. You gain darkvision out to a range of 30
feet, or extend your darkvision range by 30 feet if you
already have it. Your darkvision is not impeded by
magical darkness. While in dim light or darkness, you
can attempt to hide from creatures that would normally
perceive you through blindsense, darkvision or
tremorsense.
Heroic Perk: Death Mark

Prerequisite: Phantom Strike

You can use a bonus action to Death Mark a creature
you can see within 60 feet. While Death Marked, you
always know the creature’s exact location regardless of
distance, even across planes of existence. Your attacks
against a Death Marked creature ignore damage
resistances, and the creature cannot benefit from
invisibility or concealment against you. A Death Mark
lasts until the creature dies, you mark a new target, or
the creature is targeted by a remove curse spell or
similar magic.
Tier II: Living Shadow

Prerequisite: Death Mark

One With Darkness. While in dim light or darkness,
you can use a bonus action to become invisible. This
invisibility lasts until the start of your next turn and ends
if you attack or cast a spell.

Ghost Form. As a bonus action, you can become
incorporeal until the start of your next turn. While
incorporeal, you can move through creatures and
objects as if they were difficult terrain, you have
resistance to all nonmagical damage, and you cannot be
grappled, restrained, shoved or knocked prone. If you
end your turn inside a solid object, you take 6d6 force
damage and are pushed to the nearest unoccupied
space.

Nerve Strike. When you hit creature that cannot see
you and isn’t a Construct with a weapon attack, you can
choose to target a precise anatomical point. The
creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw
against a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your
Dexterity modifier or be stunned until the end of its next

turn. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again
until the start of your next turn.
Heroic Perk: Shroud of Night

Prerequisite: Living Shadow, Phantom Strike

You can cast the Darkness spell innately and at will,
requiring no material components. Dexterity is your
spellcasting ability for this spell. As a bonus action, you
can extend your inner shadow outward in a 60-foot
radius, turning the area to magical darkness for 1
minute. Creatures of your choice within this darkness
can see through it.
Tier III: Shadowblade

Prerequisite: Shroud of Night

Death From Nowhere. When you hit a creature that
cannot see you with a melee weapon attack, you can
cause the attack to deal double damage. You can use
this feature once per turn.

Phantom Dodge. You do not cast a shadow or a
reflection unless you choose to do so. As a reaction
when you are hit by an attack or damaged by an effect,
you can collapse into shadow entirely, causing the
attack to miss or the damage to be avoided and
teleporting you up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you
can see that is in dim light or darkness. If there are no
eligible spaces to teleport to, you cannot use this
reaction.

Path Connections
Legendary Sniper. Instead of the Death Mark Heroic Perk,
you can take the Hunter’s Eye Perk, changing to the
Legendary Sniper path.

Warlord. Instead of the Death Mark Heroic Perk, you can
take the Defensive Proficiency Perk, changing to the Warlord
path.

Original PDF page 100

Heroic Path: Sovereign

Prerequisite: Bard, Sorcerer, Paldin, or Warlock
Tier I: Regal Presence

Aura of Greatness. You emit an aura of commanding
greatness in a 120-foot radius sphere centered on you.
Friendly creatures that can see you within that radius
can add your Charisma modifier to one ability check,
attack roll, or saving throw. Once a creature has
benefited from your Aura of Greatness, that creature
cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

Sovereign Will. Your spell save DC is increased by 1.
Heroic Perk: Greater Charms

Prerequisite: Regal Presence

Your spells and class features ignore immunity to the
charmed condition.
Tier II: Crowned

Prerequisite: Greater Charms

Unassailable Mind. You are immune to the charmed
and frightened conditions. When a creature attempts to
charm or frighten you, you become aware of the attempt
and can identify the source.

Decree. As a bonus action, you issue a Decree to a
creature you can see within 60 feet. The creature must
succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell
save DC or be compelled to follow a single course of
action on its next turn, as if under the effect of the
Suggestion spell, limited to one turn. A creature that
succeeds on this saving throw is immune to your
Decree for the next 24 hours.

Presence of Command. Your presence commands
absolute social authority. You are always treated as the
highest-ranking individual in any non-hostile social
situation, regardless of titles, wealth, or station.
Creatures that would ordinarily refuse to speak with you
are compelled to hear what you have to say regardless
of their apprehensions. You always benefit from
advantage on Charisma (Persuasion, Deception and
Intimidation) checks.
Heroic Perk: Powerful Presence

Prerequisite: Crowned

Your Charisma score increases by 2, as does your
maximum for that score. Your spells and class features
ignore immunities to the frightened condition.
Tier III: Sovereign

Prerequisite: Powerful Presence

Absolute Authority. When a creature within 120 feet
of you makes a saving throw against one of your spells
or class features, you can use your reaction to force it to

fail on that saving throw. Once you use this feature, you
cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

Court of One. You are permanently under the effects
of the Glibness spell. This effect cannot be dispelled.
Additionally, creatures of your choice of CR 10 or lower
that lay eyes upon you for the first time between long
rests are charmed by you for the next 24 hours, during
which they regard you as their sovereign, follow your
commands, and will not willingly act against your
interests. This effect ends for a creature if you or your
allies deal damage to it.

Path Connections
Overlord. Instead of the Greater Charms Heroic Perk, you
can take the Thrall Perk, changing to the Overlord path.

Original PDF page 101

Heroic Path: Third Eye

Prerequisites: A Wisdom score of 20 or higher
Tier I: Knowledge of Ages

Living Memory. You have advantage on Nature,
Survival, Medicine, and Perception checks. You may
use Wisdom in place of Intelligence for any knowledge
check related to the natural world, history of places, or
ancient creatures.

Sage’s Intercession. As a bonus action on your turn,
you can invigorate an allied creature you can see within
30 feet by giving them a piece of mystical advice. That
creature can add your Wisdom modifier to an attack
roll, ability check, or saving throw they make before the
start of your next turn.
Heroic Perk: Soul of the World

Prerequisite: Knowledge of Ages

You cannot be surprised while you are conscious. You
automatically notice any changes in weather, terrain, or
ecosystems within 1 mile. You can understand, read,
and write all languages. You can communicate with
beasts, elementals, monstrosities, and other natural
creatures as though you shared a language. They
instinctively recognize you as an eminence and hold
certain reverence for you, though they are under no
compulsion to heed you if they judge your words
unsound or are otherwise hostile toward them.
Tier II: Great Keeper

Prerequisite: Soul of the World

Primordial Intellect. You gain proficiency on
Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws.

Regain Sense. As a bonus action on your turn, you can
end a single condition affecting you or an ally within 30
feet among the following: charmed, frightened, stunned,
or any condition originating from a spell of the
Enchantment school.

Wisdom of the Keeper. You learn one 8th-level spell
and one 9th-level spell from any class list. Wisdom is
your spellcasting ability for these spells.
Heroic Perk: Ascended Mind

Prerequisite: Great Keeper

Your Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores are
increased to 20, unless they are already higher without
this benefit. Scores that are already above 20 instead
increase by 1, up to a maximum of 24.

Tier III: Third Eye

Prerequisite: Ascended Mind

Foresight. Your great Wisdom allows you to predict the
near future. You are always under the effects of the
Foresight spell. This effect cannot be dispelled.

Path Connections
Archmagus. Instead of the Soul of the World Heroic Perk, you
can take the Erudition Perk, changing to the Archmagus
path.

Herald. Instead of the Ascended Mind Heroic Perk, you can
take the Words Beyond Worlds Perk, changing to the Herald
path.

Original PDF page 102

Heroic Path: Titan

Prerequisite: A Strength score of 20 or higher
Tier I: Overwhelming Might

Titanic Force. When you make an unarmed strike or
an attack with a melee weapon you are proficient with,
you ignore resistance to its damage type. Your critical
range is increased by 1 for attacks that use Strength.

Displacing Strikes. When you hit a creature with a
melee attack and that creature has a Strength score
lower than yours, you can choose to knock the creature
prone or push the creature 15 feet away. You can push
the creature another 15 feet away for each size category
smaller compared to you.

Heroic Perk: Unmatched Strength

Prerequisite: Overwhelming Might

Your Strength score increases by 2. 30 is your new
maximum for that score. You always have advantage on
Strength saving throws, ability checks, and contests.
Your carrying capacity is multiplied by 10, and you can
momentarily drag, push, pull, or carry impossible
weights–structures, ships, or crumbling mountainsides–
with a successful Athletics check with a DC at DM’s
discretion.

Tier II: Giant’s Wrath

Prerequisite: Unmatched Strength

Shockwave. When you hit a creature with a melee
attack, your attack sends a shockwave in a 15-foot
radius sphere centered on the target. Each creature in
that area other than you, including the target creature,
must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw with a DC
equal to 8 + your Strength modifier + your proficiency
bonus or take half the damage of the original attack as
thunder damage. The additional damage of the
shockwave cannot exceed 50.

Unmovable. You cannot be knocked prone, shoved, or
grappled by creatures with a lower Strength score than
you.
Heroic Perk: Siege Monster

Prerequisite: Giant’s Wrath

Your attacks deal triple damage to objects and
structures.
Tier III: Titan

Prerequisite: Siege Monster

Mythic Strength. Your Strength score increases by 4.
34 is your new maximum for that score. Your size
category is increased by one, up to a maximum of Huge.

Breaker. When you hit a creature that has 100 hit
points or less with a melee attack, they must succeed on

a Constitution saving throw with a DC equal to half the
damage dealt by the attack or be reduced to 0 hit points.

Death Grip. You can grapple a creature with your
bonus action. You can grapple creatures of any size.
When you successfully grapple a creature that has a
Constitution score of 19 or lower, you can use an action
to reduce the creature to 0 hit points.

Path Connections
Unbreakable. Instead of the Unmatched Strength Heroic
Perk, you can take the Iron Stance Perk, changing to the
Unbreakable path.

Warlord. Instead of the Siege Monster Heroic Perk, you can
take the Perfect Body Perk, changing to the Warlord path.

Original PDF page 103

Heroic Path: Unbreakable

Prerequisite: Barbarian, Fighter, Monk, or Paladin
Tier I: Greater Toughness

Stalwart Body. You are immune to poison and disease,
and you have advantage on Constitution saving throws.

Heroic Toughness. Your hit point maximum increases
by an amount equal to three times your level when you
gain this feat. Whenever you gain a level thereafter, your
hit point maximum increases by an additional 3 hit
points.

Heroic Perk: Iron Stance

Prerequisite: Greater Toughness

You cannot be knocked prone, shoved, or moved against
your will. You gain resistance to one non-legendary
damage type of your choice.
Tier II: Untarnished Blood

Prerequisite: Iron Stance

Cleanse Effect. You can use 1 LP at the start of your
turn to end all conditions or harmful effects not created
by mythical techniques or spells of 8th level or higher
currently afflicting you.

Greater Health. Your hit point maximum cannot be
reduced in any way, and you are immune to spells and
effects that would immediately reduce your hit points to
0 or kill you outright.

Heroic Perk: Stone Body

Prerequisite: Untarnished Blood

You reduce all instances of damage by 5. You have a
damage threshold of 20: whenever an instance of
damage would deal 15 or less damage to you, you take
no damage instead. Damage reductions and resistances
are considered before determining the damage
threshold of this perk.

Tier III: Unbreakable

Prerequisite: Stone Body

Unbreakable Resistance. When you fail a saving
throw, you can choose to succeed instead. After you use
this feature, you cannot use it again until you finish a
long rest.

Unbreakable Endurance. When you take an instance
of damage, it cannot exceed half of your maximum hit
points. Damage in excess of that cap is nullified.

Path Connections
Titan. Instead of the Iron Stance Heroic Perk, you can take
the Unmatched Strength Perk, changing to the Titan path.

Original PDF page 104

Heroic Path: Warlord

Prerequisite: A Strength or Dexterity score of 20 or
higher.
Tier I: Elite Skirmisher

Advantaged Strike. You have advantage on the first
attack you make on your turn.

Deflection. As a reaction when you or a creature
within range of your currently equipped melee weapon
is targeted by an attack, you can attempt to deflect it.
Make a weapon attack. If the result is higher than the
triggering strike’s attack roll, the attack misses as you
deflect it.
Heroic Perk: Technique Student

Prerequisite: Elite Skirmisher

You learn a Mythical Technique available to your class.
If you have no Mythical Techniques available to your
class, you learn the Reality Slash Mythical Technique.
Tier II: Combat Master

Prerequisite: Technique Student

Defensive Proficiency. While wielding a melee
weapon, increase your AC by 3.

Greater Critical. Your critical range is increased by 1.
When you score a critical hit, your attack ignores
resistances to its damage type.

Perfected Saves. You gain proficiency in Strength,
Dexterity, and Constitution saving throws.
Heroic Perk: Perfect Body

Prerequisite: Combat Master

Your Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution scores are
increased to 20, unless they are already higher without
this benefit. Scores that are already above 20 instead
increase by 1, up to a maximum of 24.
Tier III: Warlord

Prerequisite: Perfect Body

Mythical Technique. You learn a Mythical Technique
of your choice from any class’s list.

Technique Savant. Whenever you use a Mythical
Technique that expends 2 or more Legendary Points,
you expend 1 less Legendary Point when you use that
technique.
Original PDF page 105
